sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The row 33 looks the same. But 1 cell changed. Let me find which cell changed by comparing the full frames.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find the changed cell
curr_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
prev_lines = previous_frame.ascii.split('\n')

for i in range(len(curr_lines)):
    if i < len(prev_lines) and curr_lines[i] != prev_lines[i]:
        for j in range(len(curr_lines[i])):
            if j < len(prev_lines[i]) and curr_lines[i][j] != prev_lines[i][j]:
                print(f"Changed at row {i}, col {j}: '{prev_lines[i][j]}' -> '{curr_lines[i][j]}'")
                print(f"Context: {prev_lines[i][max(0,j-3):j+4]} -> {curr_lines[i][max(0,j-3):j+4]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Context: bbbbGGB -> bbbGGGB

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
So the change was at row 1, col 59: 'b' -> 'G'. This is in the top blue bar (the timer/progress bar at the top of the screen). This is just a HUD change, not a gameplay change. So clicking on the button at row 33, col 8 didn't actually change the button - it just changed the timer bar.
This suggests that clicking on the buttons doesn't toggle them. Maybe I need to click on the bottom squares to change the buttons.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics. In level 1, I clicked on the buttons to toggle them, and then clicked the player blob to submit. In level 2, the buttons don't seem to be directly clickable.
Looking at the bottom squares again:
- Left square (gray): has yellow dots at bottom-left
- Right square (blue): has yellow dots at top-right
The blue square is the "active" one. Maybe I need to click on the buttons on the side corresponding to the active square. Currently, the right square is blue, so I should click on the right side buttons.
But wait, I clicked on the left side button and nothing happened (except the timer). Let me try clicking on a right side button.
Actually, let me reconsider. Maybe the bottom squares work differently. When I clicked the left square earlier, the target shape on the left disappeared. When I clicked the right square, the left side's bottom vertical bars changed from w to B. This suggests that clicking a bottom square affects the OTHER side, not the same side.
Hmm, that's confusing. Let me try clicking the left bottom square again to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking the left bottom square again
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 56, 'col': 9}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
